subject: Mathematics
title: Counting Forever
description: A micro-lesson that makes Counting Forever usable.

hook.text: Start counting: 1, 2, 3. You'll never finish. There's always a next number.

buildup.text: The natural numbers go on without end. No matter how large a number, you can always add one.

discovery.text: This is potential infinity—a process that never stops rather than a completed object.

twist.text: Ancient Greeks accepted potential infinity but rejected actual infinity as absurd.

climax.text: It took over 2,000 years before mathematicians dared to treat infinity as a finished thing.

punchline.text: The simplest concept in math—the next number—leads to the deepest.

quiz.question: What is potential infinity?
options: A process that continues without end
A number larger than all others
